articles.title: ‘Impossible after 8 am’: How extreme heat is disrupting daily life across cities in Odisha - Odisha TV
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Odisha
articles.full_text:
As temperatures continue to soar across parts of Odisha, residents are struggling to cope with the growing intensity of the summer heat, with daily routines increasingly shaped by the rising mercury.
In several regions experiencing extreme conditions, stepping outdoors after early morning hours has become a challenge. People are adjusting their schedules, avoiding peak sunlight, and taking extra precautions to protect themselves from heat-related illnesses.
Daily Life Under Stress in Odisha
The impact of the heatwave is visible on streets that now remain largely deserted during the afternoon. Only essential movement continues while most people prefer to stay indoors to escape the scorching conditions.
For working professionals, especially those in physically demanding jobs, the situation has become even more difficult. Factory workers and labourers are being forced to adopt stricter safety measures, including frequent breaks and hydration, to cope with the physical strain caused by the heat.
Commuters, too, describe their routines as a ‘daily struggle’, with many finding it nearly impossible to travel during peak hours due to the harsh sun and extreme temperatures.
Staying Safe in Rising Heat
Residents are increasingly relying on simple yet crucial practices to stay safe. Carrying drinking water at all times, staying hydrated, and limiting exposure to direct sunlight have become essential habits.
Tanmaya, a resident affected by the ongoing heatwave in Sambalpur, said, “We are carrying drinking water along with us whenever we step out of the house. We constantly hydrate so that we do not get sick due to the unbearable heat. We are worried about the near future as the summer season is gradually going to peak further and result in even higher temperature.”

Concern Over What Lies Ahead
With forecasts indicating that temperatures may rise further as summer progresses, concerns are growing among citizens about how they will manage in the coming weeks.
